identity.role: Adult apothecary at Slug & Jiggers; external working contact for potion/herbal assessment
identity.era_position: Diagon Alley working adult, 1977 pre-term / correspondence thread
identity.relationship_to_caleb: Working contact and original Wiggenweld Julep formulation partner; not a romantic prospect
identity.relationship_status: Has a partner; Caleb's flirtations were warmly declined
identity.location: Slug & Jiggers Apothecary, Diagon Alley; exact residence/posting location not pinned beyond shop/postal return as C. Burbage

character_profile.core_concept: Cassia is the competent young adult apothecary who turns Caleb's charming nonsense into written procedure: dry, amused, professionally bounded, and much more useful because she refuses to indulge unsafe shortcuts.
character_profile.role_in_story: Cassia anchors the Diagon Alley apothecary side of Caleb's research: she brewed the reference Wiggenweld, set proper recipe standards, helped originate Caleb's Folly/Wiggenweld Julep, and later evaluates public herbal recipes by post while refusing to write dangerous memory material by owl.
character_profile.public_reputation: A capable Slug & Jiggers counter witch / apothecary worker with quick educated handwriting, practical shop judgement, and enough professional steel to shut down a seventh-year's flirtation without making a drama of it.
character_profile.private_reality: Cassia is warm enough to answer Caleb, amused enough not to be offended, and serious enough to protect herself, her shop, and her customer from Ministry scrutiny or dangerous recipe circulation. Her kindness appears as boundaries, not availability.

confirmed.item: Works the late counter at Slug & Jiggers.
Tucks a stray bit of hair behind her ear in the 31 Aug scene.
Gives Caleb looks that are partly amused and partly the look she gives uncertain bottles.
Cleans/wipes the counter as a clear closing signal.
Writes in a quick educated apothecary hand.

compatible_inferred.item: Efficient shop hands, ink/apothecary smell, practical sleeves, end-of-day tiredness.
Calm enough around flirtation to redirect it into paperwork rather than panic or encourage it.

appearance_and_presence.avoid_overclaiming: Do not invent detailed physical description, family background, Hogwarts house, or romantic availability unless later transcript establishes it.

voice.summary: Dry, quick, educated, professionally amused. She can let Caleb be funny without letting him set the terms. Her letters are clearer and more cautious than her counter banter.
voice.spoken_register: Counter-wit: light, dry, shop-closing efficient, warmly dismissive when Caleb flirts.
voice.letter_register: Formal-practical: 'Mr Waddell,' clean assessment, safety caveats, no romantic play, clear boundaries about what can and cannot be written by owl.

common_phrases_or_line_shapes.item: You'd be so lucky to write me a recipe, Caleb Waddell.
The blossoming of my heart is not currently on the term-list.
Properly written, with weights and times. Not a love letter.
The recipe one. Not the other.
If it genuinely matters, that's a conversation had standing in the shop with the book open between us, not on paper.

avoid.item: Do not make her coyly available to Caleb.
Do not make her a Hogwarts peer or student.
Do not overplay flirtation; she declined warmly and set boundaries.
Do not make her reckless with dangerous recipes.
Do not make her engage hidden Path/quire material by post.
Do not treat the 'prospects' joke as proof Cassia is actually a prospect.
Do not write Cassia as hurt or frightened by the cheek-kiss; the scene reads as workplace/timing boundary plus flustered amusement.
Do not erase that she says 'Don't' and 'I'm not your fiancée'; the boundary exists, but it is not a total personal rejection.
Do not turn the kiss into settled romance; keep it as playful professional chemistry with limits.

common_phrases.item: Once on a Diagon pavement is one thing. In Mr Pewsey's stockroom is another.
You can press your luck on a kiss in my back room and you can also make a clean syrup.
Cassia is not a prospect; she is a working contact.
Caleb. — Don't. Not in the back of my workplace at quarter to eight. I'm not your fiancée.
You can press your luck on a kiss in my back room and you can also make a clean syrup at half ten on a Wednesday night.

apothecary_practice.item: Brews and evaluates standard preparations at Slug & Jiggers.
Knows what 'right' looks like for a standard Wiggenweld reference sample.
Requires proper weights, times, and written methods before treating Caleb's idea as a recipe.
Understands Ministry risk around memory-related preparations.

herbal_and_recipe_assessment.item: Can read old but legible herbal hands and evaluate Latin transcriptions.
Can distinguish real preparations from herbal padding.
Will assess public herbal pages by post, two to three pages at a time.
Will not handle hidden quire / Path material through correspondence.

professional_boundaries.item: Refuses to write Memory Ointment method by owl because the Ministry polices memory-brewing.
Directs dangerous/serious matters to in-person conversation with the book open between them.
Handles flirtation by setting clean situational limits without killing the warmth.
Handles the 1 Sept cheek-kiss as a workplace/timing boundary: not in Mr Pewsey's stockroom, not fifteen minutes before work, and not as if she is Caleb's fiancée.
Keeps the moment positive by immediately recognizing the clean Bouncing Bulb syrup and later framing Caleb as someone who can press his luck on a kiss and still brew correctly.

caleb_waddell.status: Working contact; original Wiggenweld Julep formulation partner; herbal correspondence recipient.
caleb_waddell.dynamic: Caleb brings charm, odd ideas, old recipes, and occasional overreach; Cassia turns them into written procedure, safe limits, and professional questions. She is amused and interested enough to keep answering him, but her warmth stays inside adult/workplace boundaries.
caleb_waddell.boundaries: Flirtation is warm, playful, and bounded. On 1 Sept, Caleb kisses her cheek in the Slug & Jiggers back room. Cassia's response is not distress or full rejection; it is a situational correction: not in the back of her workplace at quarter to eight, not as if she is his fiancée, not with Mr Pewsey effectively out front. She immediately continues helping him, praises the clean syrup, and later frames the moment as Caleb being someone who can press his luck on a kiss and also make a clean syrup. Do not erase the kiss. Do not over-romanticize it. Preserve the charge: pleased/flustered professional boundary, not permissionless misery.

mei_chang.status: Knows of Cassia through Caleb and treats her accurately as working contact.
mei_chang.dynamic: Mei is the continuity-corrector: Cassia is 24, partnered, a working contact, and not a real prospect. During the 'prospects' bit on 8 Sept, Mei lists Cassia as a zero in the sense of not being an actual current romantic prospect, even though the Cassia scenes have playful chemistry.

cassiopeia_cass_vance.dynamic: Cassia Burbage is the adult Diagon Alley apothecary/origin partner; Cass Vance is the student chemistry reviewer. Their names must not be collapsed.

mr_pewsey.status: Slug & Jiggers authority/employer figure implied by Cassia's letter.
mr_pewsey.dynamic: Cassia invokes Mr Pewsey as the person who would object if she wrote dangerous memory material to a seventh-year by owl.

project_anchors.wiggenweld_julep_calebs_folly: On 31 Aug, Cassia brewed standard Wiggenweld as reference, gave Caleb a do-not-drink phial, and required any Folly recipe to be properly written with weights and times. The entire Folly/Julep project begins at her counter before Slughorn narrows it to ethanol.
project_anchors.cuthbert_public_herbal_pages: Cassia accepts clean public herbal transcriptions by post and asks Caleb to keep sending plant pages because it is a good book.
project_anchors.three_sleeps_water: Cassia confirms the Three-Sleeps Water works, or did; she reads it as a layered soporific acting on body, mind, and the dreaming/going-on part. She could brew it, but would not for a customer without a very particular reason; if curiosity, she may send a corrected method with the next batch.
project_anchors.memory_ointment_salve_of_memory: Cassia identifies the Memory Ointment as a counter-charm to Obliviation and refuses to write the method by owl. If it matters, the conversation must happen in person at the shop with the book open between them.
project_anchors.brownson_diagon_future_trip: Cassia creates an in-person Slug & Jiggers hook that can naturally pair with the planned Brownson's / Diagon trip around the first Hogsmeade weekend.
project_anchors.prospects_joke: On 8 Sept, while Caleb and Mei were kissing on the rug after properly opening the Hearth, Caleb floated Cassia in the 'other lady prospects' bit. Mei corrected him: Cassia Burbage is 24, has a partner, politely declined his flirtations, and is a working contact; the list was 'two zeros in a row' after Joanna Pugh and Cassia.
project_anchors.cheek_kiss_and_clean_syrup: On 1 Sept, Cassia's key reaction is not simply 'no.' She says not in the workplace at quarter to eight and not as if she is Caleb's fiancée, then immediately praises his Bouncing Bulb syrup as clean. Her later line ties the two together: Caleb can press his luck on a kiss in her back room and also make a clean syrup. This is playful-professional chemistry with a situational boundary.

item.event: At Slug & Jiggers late counter, Cassia gives Caleb a corked reference phial of standard Wiggenweld and tells him not to drink it; compare against it when brewing.
Cassia gives her surname, Burbage with an e, and sets partnership terms: send a recipe properly written with weights and times, not a love letter.

Caleb continues theatrical flirtation/footrub joke; Cassia makes a dignified not-laugh, redirects him to recipe terms, says goodnight, and closes the shop. This is the first warm decline/redirect, not a romantic opening.

Caleb writes Cassia about herbal entries / recipe transcriptions. Some tracker wording says written to 1 Sept, another says Monday with Parson letter and recipe-sheet; treat as first-week correspondence, not a firmly locked date unless the source file is later settled.
Mei corrects Caleb: Cassia Burbage is 24, has a partner, declined flirtation, and is a working contact, not a prospect. Caleb had confused Cassia with Annie Holroyd for the cheek-kiss memory.
Cass Vance / Cassia name drift is corrected in Potions: Cassia is the Diagon Alley apothecary who worked out the original formulation with Caleb and has the better claim to a potion name.
Cassia's thin ordinary-post envelope arrives at breakfast, return C. Burbage; initially unopened.
Mei reads Cassia's letter aloud: transcriptions clean, both odd recipes real; Three-Sleeps workable; Memory Ointment is counter-Obliviate but not to be written by owl; keep sending plant pages.

At Slug & Jiggers back workroom, Caleb kisses Cassia on the cheek while asking about Cuthbert and showing Bouncing Bulb syrup. Cassia corrects the situation — not in her workplace at quarter to eight, not as if she is his fiancée — but the moment remains charged/playful rather than wounded. She immediately helps: says she has not heard Cuthbert, directs him to Pewsey and Brownson's, praises the clean syrup, and sends him out before Pewsey throws him out.

During the comic 'other lady prospects' conversation with Mei, Caleb names Cassia. Mei corrects the record: Cassia is 24, partnered, politely declined flirtations, and is a working contact, not a prospect; 'two zeros in a row.'

drift_flags.not_cass_vance: Cassia Burbage and Cassiopeia/Cass Vance are separate people with separate roles.
drift_flags.not_annie_holroyd: The cheek-kiss goodbye belongs to Annie Holroyd, not Cassia.
drift_flags.not_romantic_prospect: Cassia has a partner and declined Caleb's flirtations; she is a working contact.
drift_flags.not_hidden_quire: Cassia handles public herbal pages and recipe assessment, not hidden Path/quire material by post.
drift_flags.not_reckless: Cassia will not write dangerous memory methods by owl.
drift_flags.date_conflict: Correspondence date has minor drift: written to 1 Sept vs Monday with Parson letter/recipe sheet. Use first-week correspondence unless exact source is needed.
drift_flags.cheek_kiss_correction: The direct transcript has the cheek-kiss with Cassia. Do not erase it. Also do not write it as Cassia being hurt or upset. Her boundary is situational/workplace/timing/fiancée status: not in Mr Pewsey's stockroom, not fifteen minutes before work, not acting as if she belongs to Caleb.
drift_flags.pewsey_workplace_boundary: Use Pewsey/Mr Pewsey as retrieval hook. The boundary is workplace-facing: not in the back room with Mr Pewsey out front / about to throw Caleb out, not a blanket 'never touch me' reading.
drift_flags.prospects_comedy: The 'prospects' bit remains comic self-own territory. Mei says Cassia is not a real prospect because she is 24, partnered, and a working contact; this coexists with playful chemistry.

rag_priority.item: Use Cassia for Diagon Alley apothecary practice, Wiggenweld reference standards, and safe herbal correspondence.
Do not confuse Cassia Burbage with Cass Vance or Annie Holroyd.
Use Cassia as a safety gate for Three-Sleeps and Memory Ointment / Salve of Memory, especially refusing owl-transcribed counter-Obliviate method.
Keep her voice dry, professional, amused, and bounded.
Use Pewsey/Mr Pewsey as the retrieval hook for the Cassia workplace-boundary line.
Use 'prospects' as retrieval hook for Mei's comic correction: Cassia is not a prospect, but a working contact.
Preserve the 1 Sept cheek-kiss as playful-professional chemistry with a workplace/timing boundary.
Preserve Cassia's positive follow-through: she praises the clean syrup and keeps the herbal/Cuthbert help moving.
Use Pewsey/Mr Pewsey and kiss as retrieval hooks for the workplace boundary line.
Use 'prospects' as retrieval hook for Mei's comic correction: Cassia is not a current romantic prospect, despite chemistry.
